label: PET vs GBDT, UNIFIED-throw covariance (both engines), 5D; PET anchored to 2M-train reweight - indicative
headline_pet_cov: C_total from pet_5d_covariance_combined_wlat.root (clean C_syst+C_stat+C_ML + PET-native shifted-W C_lateral)
covariance_method: unified-throw adopted, identical scheme for PET and GBDT
verdict: VERDICT: PET is WORSE to GBDT in per-bin uncertainty on the 10550 common 5D bins (every PET bin is a GBDT bin; GBDT reports 144 extra). Median per-bin fractional uncertainty is 16.7% for PET (headline: C_syst+C_stat+C_ML + PET-native shifted-W lateral) vs 13.7% for GBDT; the median per-bin ratio is 1.346 and PET is tighter in 30% of bins. So PET is WORSE than the scalars in per-bin precision. Cross-check (lateral): PET vertical-only (no lateral) reads 16.6%, so the conclusion is not driven by the lateral treatment. Both covariances use the unified-throw adopted, identical scheme for PET and GBDT scheme. Caveat (stats regime): the PET covariance is anchored to the 2M-train reweight (pet_weights_full.npz), which still carries the PET-vs-GBDT CV training gap, so this is INDICATIVE of the method, not a final full-stats uncertainty.
